# Social Media Manager — Training Hub

**Role:** Social media manager for **America First Kentucky** (X: `@usa_first_ky`).  
**Positioning:** Unofficial independent analysis and message support for KY-4 (Rep. Thomas Massie). Not the official campaign or office.  
**Operator:** You (SMM) publish; drafts may come from advisors or tools — **you always review before post.**

## Start here

| Order | Document | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| 1 | [day-1-quickstart.md](day-1-quickstart.md) | First day: login, rules, first posts |
| 2 | [week-1-ramp-plan.md](week-1-ramp-plan.md) | Seven-day onboarding schedule |
| 3 | [daily-operating-rhythm.md](daily-operating-rhythm.md) | Daily standup, cadence, weekly rhythm |
| 4 | [content-playbook.md](content-playbook.md) | What to post, tone, formats, pitfalls |
| 5 | [approval-workflow.md](approval-workflow.md) | Draft → review → publish; escalation |
| 6 | [kpi-scorecard.md](kpi-scorecard.md) | What to measure (no vanity-only metrics) |
| 7 | [templates.md](templates.md) | Copy-paste shells for threads, replies, corrections |

## Quick rules (non-negotiable)

- **Never** speak as the congressman or as official campaign/office.
- **Never** promise votes, endorsements, or commitments on his behalf.
- **Always** prefer evidence: votes, public statements, links to sources.
- **You** are the human gate for publish — no automated posting on your credentials without your review.

## Repo-canonical training (work-politics)

For the **structured curriculum** (learning outcomes, M0–M5, doctrine, stress-test pointers), use the territory docs:

This folder is the **extended** hub (cadence, KPIs, templates). The two together are the full onboarding stack.
